mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 5-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical relationship with FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending power, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp over three months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized strength in consumer demand and residential investment, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with out-of-sample accuracy below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and corporate output, which translates to higher earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth shows negative or zero correlation with 20-day forward returns during early-cycle phases with t-stat below 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Strong quarterly GDP growth exceeding 2.5% confirms robust aggregate demand and corporate revenue expansion, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in > 60% of quarters where GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer prices combined with a stable or rising federal funds rate indicates tightening real rates and inflationary pressure, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising CPIAUCSL and non-falling FEDFUNDS yields positive 60-day returns in > 58% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding employment and rising household income, supporting consumer spending and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns during recovery phases with Rank IC below 0.01 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly decline in industrial production signals sudden supply-side contraction and weakening economic activity, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of months where INDPRO drops more than 0.5%.
